turns.content:
You are the subagent Name:gsd-phase-researcher role: OpenCode SDK/primitives research specialist. You must do as this prompt instructs and know that you are being delegated by the front-facing orchestrator.

Task boundary: research external/current OpenCode SDK and primitive behavior relevant to upgrading `execute-slash-command`. Do not modify files. Do not propose implementation yet. Return evidence and unanswered questions.

Mandatory source context from user:
- `.hivemind/STACKS-REFERENCES.md` states active OpenCode repo is `anomalyco/opencode`, packages `@opencode-ai/sdk` and `@opencode-ai/plugin` at npm latest 1.14.44; legacy `opencode-ai/opencode` is archived.
- `repo-for-learning-and-synthesis.md` says synthesize patterns toward Hivemind philosophy, not copy wholesale; notable repos: `Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning`, `shekohex/opencode-pty`, `kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents`, `awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode`.

Research requirements:
- Use internet-backed MCP/repo tools where available: DeepWiki/GitHub/Context7/Repomix/Exa/Tavily or equivalent. Validate against active repo `anomalyco/opencode`, not archived legacy.
- Investigate OpenCode SDK/plugin capabilities for session agent switching, TUI prompt/append command execution, command discovery, command file locations (`.opencode/command` vs `.opencode/commands`, `.opencode/agent` vs `.opencode/agents`, project/global), command frontmatter fields such as `agent`, `subtask`, argument substitution (`$ARGUMENTS`), and task/subagent delegation semantics.
- Investigate patterns in the suggested OpenCode ecosystem repos only as synthesis references; do not assume they are directly portable.
- Include URLs/repo paths and exact evidence snippets where possible.
- Return compact report in Vietnamese prose with source citations/links.

Expected return sections:
1. OpenCode SDK/plugin surfaces relevant to agent switching and command execution
2. Primitive layout and frontmatter semantics discovered
3. `$ARGUMENTS`/flags/reference parsing behavior discovered
4. Ecosystem repo patterns that may inform but not dictate Hivemind design
5. Uncertainties requiring follow-up codebase validation

Tôi là **subagent gsd-phase-researcher**. Báo cáo dưới đây chỉ là **research evidence**, không sửa file và không đề xuất implementation chi tiết.

## 1. OpenCode SDK/plugin surfaces liên quan agent switching + command execution

- **Active source verified:** `anomalyco/opencode`, tag `v1.14.44`, không dùng repo legacy archived.  
  - SDK/client source: [`packages/sdk/js/src/v2/client.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/sdk/js/src/v2/client.ts)
  - Generated SDK: [`packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts)

- **NPM discrepancy:** user context nói latest `1.14.44`, nhưng `npm view @opencode-ai/sdk` và `@opencode-ai/plugin` hiện trả latest `1.15.7`. Vì task yêu cầu validate `1.14.44`, tôi dùng tag `v1.14.44`; planner nên kiểm tra project pin thực tế trước khi lock. `[VERIFIED: npm registry + GitHub tag]`

- SDK v2 có các surface quan trọng:
  - `client.session.prompt(...)` → `POST /session/{sessionID}/message`; body có `agent`, `model`, `variant`, `parts`, `tools`. Evidence snippet: generated method `public prompt... url: "/session/{sessionID}/message"`.
  - `client.session.command(...)` → `POST /session/{sessionID}/command`; body có `agent`, `model`, `arguments`, `command`, `variant`, `parts`. Evidence snippet: generated method `public command... url: "/session/{sessionID}/command"`.
  - `client.command.list(...)` → `GET /command` for command discovery. Evidence snippet: `export class Command ... public list ... url: "/command"`.
  - `client.tui.appendPrompt(...)` → `POST /tui/append-prompt`; only appends prompt text.
  - `client.tui.executeCommand(...)` → `POST /tui/execute-command`; executes TUI command string, not necessarily same semantics as session slash-command execution.  
  Sources: generated SDK snippets from [`sdk.gen.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts), Context7 `/anomalyco/opencode-sdk-js`.

- **Agent switching:** `SessionPrompt.createUserMessage()` chooses `input.agent || defaultAgent`, validates agent exists, stores `info.agent`, and emits `SessionEvent.AgentSwitched` when stored session agent differs. Source: [`packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts).  
  Evidence snippet: `const agentName = input.agent || (yield* agents.defaultAgent())`; later `EventV2.run(SessionEvent.AgentSwitched.Sync, { ... agent: info.agent })`.

- **Plugin hook surface:** `Plugin.Service.trigger()` invokes registered hooks sequentially. Source: [`packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts).  
  In command flow, OpenCode triggers `command.execute.before` with `{ command, sessionID, arguments }` and mutable `{ parts }`. Source: `session/prompt.ts`.

## 2. Primitive layout + frontmatter semantics discovered

- **Commands support BOTH singular and plural directories in v1.14.44:**  
  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/config/command.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/config/command.ts)  
  Evidence snippet: `Glob.scan("{command,commands}/**/*.md", ...)` and patterns include `"/.opencode/command/"`, `"/.opencode/commands/"`, `"/command/"`, `"/commands/"`.

- **Agents also support BOTH singular and plural directories in v1.14.44:**  
  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/config/agent.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/config/agent.ts)  
  Evidence snippet: `Glob.scan("{agent,agents}/**/*.md", ...)` and patterns include `"/.opencode/agent/"`, `"/.opencode/agents/"`, `"/agent/"`, `"/agents/"`.

- **Command frontmatter/config schema:**  
  Source: `config/command.ts`  
  Evidence snippet:
  ```ts
  export const Info = Schema.Struct({
    template: Schema.String,
    description: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
    agent: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
    model: Schema.optional(ConfigModelID),
    subtask: Schema.optional(Schema.Boolean),
  })
  ```

- **Agent frontmatter/config schema:**  
  Source: `config/agent.ts`  
  Supports `model`, `variant`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `prompt`, deprecated `tools`, `disable`, `description`, `mode: "subagent" | "primary" | "all"`, `hidden`, `options`, `color`, `steps/maxSteps`, `permission`.

- **Command registry sources:**  
  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts)  
  Registry merges:
  1. built-in `init` and `review`;
  2. `cfg.command`;
  3. MCP prompts;
  4. skills as commands if no name collision.

## 3. `$ARGUMENTS` / flags / reference parsing behavior discovered

- Command argument parsing is in `SessionPrompt.command()`. Source: [`session/prompt.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts).  
  Evidence snippets:
  - `const argsRegex = /(?:\[Image\s+\d+\]|"[^"]*"|'[^']*'|[^\s"']+)/gi`
  - `const placeholderRegex = /\$(\d+)/g`
  - `const quoteTrimRegex = /^["']|["']$/g`

- Behavior:
  - `$1`, `$2`, ... are replaced by parsed positional args.
  - The **last numbered placeholder absorbs the remaining args**: `if (position === last) return args.slice(argIndex).join(" ")`.
  - `$ARGUMENTS` is replaced with raw `input.arguments`.
  - If no `$1` placeholders and no `$ARGUMENTS`, but args exist, OpenCode appends args to the template: `template = template + "\n\n" + input.arguments`.
  - Shell interpolation exists via backtick syntax: `!`cmd`` matched by `bashRegex = /!`([^`]+)`/g`, executed with preferred shell, output replaces the marker.
  - File/agent references are resolved by `resolvePromptParts()`: markdown file references become file parts; if no file exists and an agent name matches, an `agent` part is added.

- **Subtask semantics for commands:**  
  `isSubtask = (agent.mode === "subagent" && cmd.subtask !== false) || cmd.subtask === true`.  
  If subtask, OpenCode creates a `SubtaskPart` with target agent/model/prompt, while the user message uses current/default agent; run loop then handles the subtask through native `task` tool.

- **Native task semantics:**  
  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts)  
  Evidence:
  - `task_id` resumes previous subagent session.
  - New subtask creates child session with `parentID: ctx.sessionID`.
  - It uses target agent model if configured, else inherits current assistant model/provider.

## 4. Ecosystem repo patterns that inform but do not dictate Hivemind design

- **Opencode-DCP / dynamic-context-pruning**  
  Source: [`Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning`](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning) + Exa release result.  
  Useful pattern: expose one unified model-facing tool (`compress`) and slash commands for manual control; protect important tool outputs; do not mutate raw session history directly, instead transform context sent to model. Not directly portable, but useful for “single narrow surface + durable policy” design.

- **kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents**  
  Source: [`kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents`](https://github.com/kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents), OCX docs.  
  Useful pattern: async delegation has stable IDs, explicit lifecycle states, persistence-before-notify, compaction carry-forward, and read-only/write-capable split. It explicitly says write-capable agents should use native `task` because background sessions cannot preserve undo/branching parity.

- **shekohex/opencode-pty**  
  Source: [`shekohex/opencode-pty`](https://github.com/shekohex/opencode-pty) README via Tavily extract.  
  Useful pattern: long-running PTY work is exposed through tools plus slash commands (`/pty-open-background-spy`, `/pty-show-server-url`) and a web observer UI. Important warning: agent prompting must name background work as “session” to avoid shell `&` misuse.

- **awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode**  
  Source: [`awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode`](https://github.com/awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode), `data/plugins/background-agents.yaml`.  
  Useful only as ecosystem index; it confirms existence/category of plugins like Background Agents and DCP but is not behavioral authority.

## 5. Uncertainties requiring follow-up codebase validation

1. **Project pin vs npm latest conflict:** npm latest is `1.15.7`, while provided stack context says `1.14.44`. Need local `package.json`/lockfile validation before planner chooses exact API target.

2. **TUI `executeCommand` semantics:** SDK exposes `client.tui.executeCommand({ command })`, but evidence suggests session slash commands should use `client.session.command(...)` for command templates, `$ARGUMENTS`, `agent`, `model`, and `subtask`. Need local `execute-slash-command` code validation to see whether it currently uses TUI append/execute because of runtime constraints.

3. **Plugin availability of `client.session.command`:** Plugin receives `createOpencodeClient(...)` in `PluginInput`; source confirms client exists, but local Hivemind wrapper may use older SDK types or its own session API wrapper. Need local compile/type validation.

4. **Global/project precedence exact order:** Source confirms singular/plural scanning inside each config dir, but exact merge order across global, custom config dir, project config should be validated in `Config` loader if planner needs overwrite rules.

5. **Command names from nested paths:** `configEntryNameFromPath(...)` determines names for nested command files. Need inspect if Hivemind uses nested command namespaces or flat names.

  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts)  
  Registry merges:
  1. built-in `init` and `review`;
  2. `cfg.command`;
  3. MCP prompts;
  4. skills as commands if no name collision.

## 3. `$ARGUMENTS` / flags / reference parsing behavior discovered

- Command argument parsing is in `SessionPrompt.command()`. Source: [`session/prompt.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts).  
  Evidence snippets:
  - `const argsRegex = /(?:\[Image\s+\d+\]|"[^"]*"|'[^']*'|[^\s"']+)/gi`
  - `const placeholderRegex = /\$(\d+)/g`
  - `const quoteTrimRegex = /^["']|["']$/g`

- Behavior:
  - `$1`, `$2`, ... are replaced by parsed positional args.
  - The **last numbered placeholder absorbs the remaining args**: `if (position === last) return args.slice(argIndex).join(" ")`.
  - `$ARGUMENTS` is replaced with raw `input.arguments`.
  - If no `$1` placeholders and no `$ARGUMENTS`, but args exist, OpenCode appends args to the template: `template = template + "\n\n" + input.arguments`.
  - Shell interpolation exists via backtick syntax: `!`cmd`` matched by `bashRegex = /!`([^`]+)`/g`, executed with preferred shell, output replaces the marker.
  - File/agent references are resolved by `resolvePromptParts()`: markdown file references become file parts; if no file exists and an agent name matches, an `agent` part is added.

- **Subtask semantics for commands:**  
  `isSubtask = (agent.mode === "subagent" && cmd.subtask !== false) || cmd.subtask === true`.  
  If subtask, OpenCode creates a `SubtaskPart` with target agent/model/prompt, while the user message uses current/default agent; run loop then handles the subtask through native `task` tool.

- **Native task semantics:**  
  Source: [`packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts`](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/v1.14.44/packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts)  
  Evidence:
  - `task_id` resumes previous subagent session.
  - New subtask creates child session with `parentID: ctx.sessionID`.
  - It uses target agent model if configured, else inherits current assistant model/provider.

## 4. Ecosystem repo patterns that inform but do not dictate Hivemind design

- **Opencode-DCP / dynamic-context-pruning**  
  Source: [`Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning`](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning) + Exa release result.  
  Useful pattern: expose one unified model-facing tool (`compress`) and slash commands for manual control; protect important tool outputs; do not mutate raw session history directly, instead transform context sent to model. Not directly portable, but useful for “single narrow surface + durable policy” design.

- **kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents**  
  Source: [`kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents`](https://github.com/kdcokenny/opencode-background-agents), OCX docs.  
  Useful pattern: async delegation has stable IDs, explicit lifecycle states, persistence-before-notify, compaction carry-forward, and read-only/write-capable split. It explicitly says write-capable agents should use native `task` because background sessions cannot preserve undo/branching parity.

- **shekohex/opencode-pty**  
  Source: [`shekohex/opencode-pty`](https://github.com/shekohex/opencode-pty) README via Tavily extract.  
  Useful pattern: long-running PTY work is exposed through tools plus slash commands (`/pty-open-background-spy`, `/pty-show-server-url`) and a web observer UI. Important warning: agent prompting must name background work as “session” to avoid shell `&` misuse.

- **awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode**  
  Source: [`awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode`](https://github.com/awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode), `data/plugins/background-agents.yaml`.  
  Useful only as ecosystem index; it confirms existence/category of plugins like Background Agents and DCP but is not behavioral authority.

## 5. Uncertainties requiring follow-up codebase validation

1. **Project pin vs npm latest conflict:** npm latest is `1.15.7`, while provided stack context says `1.14.44`. Need local `package.json`/lockfile validation before planner chooses exact API target.

2. **TUI `executeCommand` semantics:** SDK exposes `client.tui.executeCommand({ command })`, but evidence suggests session slash commands should use `client.session.command(...)` for command templates, `$ARGUMENTS`, `agent`, `model`, and `subtask`. Need local `execute-slash-command` code validation to see whether it currently uses TUI append/execute because of runtime constraints.

3. **Plugin availability of `client.session.command`:** Plugin receives `createOpencodeClient(...)` in `PluginInput`; source confirms client exists, but local Hivemind wrapper may use older SDK types or its own session API wrapper. Need local compile/type validation.

4. **Global/project precedence exact order:** Source confirms singular/plural scanning inside each config dir, but exact merge order across global, custom config dir, project config should be validated in `Config` loader if planner needs overwrite rules.

5. **Command names from nested paths:** `configEntryNameFromPath(...)` determines names for nested command files. Need inspect if Hivemind uses nested command namespaces or flat names.
